free_text: Nuclear reactions in Rb,Sr,Y and Zr targets
Pure (99.9% or 3N) target foils of Y,Zr and in some
gases vapor deposited Sr were stacked with monitor
and guard aluminum (5N) foils, each being about 20 mum.
Exact thickness was determined by weighting
Louvain-La-Neuve (59,75 and 80 MeV)
Orsay-SC (150,168 and 200 MeV)
Saturne-II (1 and 2.5 GeV)
CERN-PS (24 GeV)
Total uncertainty includes:
Error in homogeneity and purity of
target foils
Errors associated with monitoring were
2-4% for counting of Na
uncertainty in monitor reaction
cross section.
Uncertainty in the efficiency of the
detector
Uncertainty in the photopeak integration
and statistical errors
Numerous corrections in BIB and DATA
sections
Some reaction lines are corrected
DATE is corrected
Subent 009 is corrected according
N.Otsuka remarks.
SD: Corrections in all Subents. ERR-ANALYS
updated. Subent 066 deleted.
Shielded.

DATA-ERR -> ERR-T. STATUS added.
Several months after irradiation targets and
blank foils were stored (ten a time) in an extraction
system in which an ultrahigh vacuum (better than
10**(-9) Torr) was progressively attained by pumping
and baking all elements. Targets were baked several
days at 80. to 100. degr.C and authors checked that
this procedure did not outgas any spallogenic
krypton. Each foil was then melted inside a
molybdenum crucible heated by an electronic
bombardment oven routinely operating at 1900.degr.C.
Extracted gases were exposed in two step to Ti and
Cu-O-Pd getters between 700. and 200.degr.C for
eliminating active species. Krypton(95-98%) was then
adsorbed on a first activated charcoal trap at -120
degr. The remainder was adsorbed together with 98-99%
argon on a second trap at -196 degr.C. Argon and
krypton were successively introduced into mass
spectrometer.
Tbl.V from Phys.Rev.,C26(1982)931
DATA-ERR -> ERR-T. STATUS added.

adsorbed on a first activated charcoal trap at -120
degr. The remainder was adsorbed together with 98-99%
argon on a second trap at -196 degr.C. Argon and
krypton were successively introduced into mass
spectrometer.

SD: DATA-ERR -> ERR-T.
Several months after irradiation targets and
blank foils were stored (ten a time) in an extraction
system in which an ultrahigh vacuum (better than
10**(-9) Torr) was progressively attained by pumping
and baking all elements. Targets were baked several
days at 80. to 100. degr.C and authors checked that
this procedure did not outgas any spallogenic
krypton. Each foil was then melted inside a
molybdenum crucible heated by an electronic
bombardment oven routinely operating at 1900.degr.C.
Extracted gases were exposed in two step to Ti and
Cu-O-Pd getters between 700. and 200.degr.C for
eliminating active species. Krypton(95-98%) was then
adsorbed on a first activated charcoal trap at -120
degr. The remainder was adsorbed together with 98-99%
argon on a second trap at -196 degr.C. Argon and
krypton were successively introduced into mass
spectrometer.
A Vapor-deposited Sr target,0.16 mg/cm**2 thick
All results average two to four independent measurement
weighted by individual errors.
From authors: Unfortunately, the deposit broke during
preparation or handling of the stack, so that only the
isotopic composition of spallation produced krypton
could be obtained.
